---
title: "bnlearn: Lock treatment as a root + prevent arrows from the response"
format: html
---

## Goal

When learning a DAG with `bnlearn` (e.g., via hill-climbing `hc()`), it’s often helpful in ecology experiments to:

1. **Lock the treatment as a root node** (no arrows into treatment).
2. **Prevent arrows from the response variable** (response has no outgoing arrows).

This stabilizes search by encoding what you already know from design/causal logic:
- Experimental treatment is assigned (nothing causes it in your dataset).
- The response is an endpoint (it doesn’t cause predictors measured earlier).

---

## Copy code

```{r}
library(bnlearn)

# ----------------------------
# 1) Define nodes (must match df column names exactly)
# ----------------------------
nodes <- c(
  "edge_dist",
  "canopy",
  "temp_c",
  "predator_playback",     # treatment
  "flock_size",
  "provisioning_visits",   # response
  "age_class",
  "sex"
)

treatment <- "predator_playback"
response  <- "provisioning_visits"

# Ensure df has these columns
stopifnot(all(nodes %in% names(df)))
df <- df[, nodes, drop = FALSE]

# ----------------------------
# 2) Ensure bnlearn-supported data types
#    - discrete: factor / ordered
#    - continuous: numeric (double)
# ----------------------------
# (Adjust to your reality; this is a common setup)
df$treatment <- NULL  # (do nothing; placeholder to avoid accidental use)

# Typical: treatment/sex/age are discrete; others numeric; response numeric or discretized
df[[treatment]] <- factor(df[[treatment]])
df[["sex"]]     <- factor(df[["sex"]])
df[["age_class"]] <- factor(df[["age_class"]])

# If response is integer counts, bnlearn rejects "integer" class; convert
df[[response]] <- as.numeric(df[[response]])

# Ensure key continuous covariates are numeric (not integer/character)
cont_vars <- setdiff(nodes, c(treatment, "sex", "age_class"))
df[cont_vars] <- lapply(df[cont_vars], function(x) as.numeric(x))

# ----------------------------
# 3) Build constraints
#    A) Lock treatment as root: forbid all incoming arcs -> treatment
#    B) Prevent outgoing arcs from response: forbid response -> anything
# ----------------------------
incoming_to_treatment <- setdiff(nodes, treatment)
outgoing_from_response <- setdiff(nodes, response)

blacklist_treatment_root <- data.frame(
  from = incoming_to_treatment,
  to   = rep(treatment, length(incoming_to_treatment)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

blacklist_no_response_outgoing <- data.frame(
  from = rep(response, length(outgoing_from_response)),
  to   = outgoing_from_response,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

blacklist <- unique(rbind(blacklist_treatment_root, blacklist_no_response_outgoing))

# Optional sanity checks
stopifnot(!anyNA(blacklist))
stopifnot(all(blacklist$from %in% nodes), all(blacklist$to %in% nodes))
stopifnot(all(blacklist$from != blacklist$to))   # no self-loops

# ----------------------------
# 4) Learn the structure (mixed data => conditional Gaussian score)
# ----------------------------
bn <- hc(
  df,
  score     = "bic-cg",
  blacklist = blacklist
)

bn
arcs(bn)

# Optional: plot (requires Rgraphviz)
library(Rgraphviz) 
graphviz.plot(bn)

# ----------------------------
# 5) (Optional) Fit parameters after structure learning
# ----------------------------
fit <- bn.fit(bn, df, method = "mle")
fit

```
